title: Self-balancing board with powered auxiliary wheels
abstract: A self-balancing board (10) is provided, having a primary wheel assembly (14) coupled to a platform (11). The primary wheel assembly (14) has a primary wheel (38). An orientation sensor system (27) is coupled to the platform (11) and configured to determine the orientation of at least one of the first foot deck portion (13a) and the second foot deck portion (13b) relative to horizontal. At least one auxiliary wheel assembly (16) is coupled to the platform (11) and is elevated above a horizontal flat surface upon which the primary wheel (38) is positioned when the platform (11) is parallel to horizontal, the at least one auxiliary wheel assembly (16) being positioned towards a distal end of the one of the first foot deck portion (13a) and the second foot deck portion (13b) and contacting the flat surface to prevent contact of the distal end of the one of the first foot deck portion (13a) and second foot deck portion (13b) with the horizontal flat surface when the distal end moves towards the horizontal flat surface. At least one motor (20) is configured to drive at least a portion of the primary wheel assembly (14) and the at least one auxiliary wheel assembly (16) at least partially based on sensor data from the orientation sensor system (27).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A self-balancing board, comprising: a primary wheel assembly having a primary wheel; a platform coupled to the primary wheel assembly, the platform having a first foot deck portion extending in a first direction from the primary wheel assembly, and a second foot deck portion extending in a second direction from the primary wheel assembly; an orientation sensor system coupled to the platform and configured to determine the orientation of at least one of the first foot deck portion and the second foot deck portion relative to horizontal; at least one auxiliary wheel assembly coupled to the platform and being elevated above a horizontal flat surface upon which the primary wheel is positioned when the platform is parallel to horizontal, the at least one auxiliary wheel assembly being positioned towards a distal longitudinal end of the platform and contacting the flat surface to prevent contact of the distal end of the platform with the horizontal flat surface when the distal end moves towards the horizontal flat surface; at least one motor configured to drive at least a portion of the primary wheel assembly and the at least one auxiliary wheel assembly; and a controller in communication with the orientation sensor system, and configured to drive the at least one motor at least partially based on the orientation of the platform. 2. A self-balancing board as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: a contact sensor system coupled to the at least one auxiliary wheel assembly and configured to determine when the at least one auxiliary wheel assembly is in contact with a travel surface, and wherein the controller is in communication with the contact sensor system, and is configured to drive the at least one motor when the orientation sensor system detects that the orientation of the platform is within a normal operating range and the contact sensor system detects that the at least one auxiliary wheel assembly is in contact with the travel surface. 3. A self-balancing board as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the primary wheel assembly has a first wheel portion, and a second wheel portion sharing a rotation axis with the first wheel portion. 4. A self-balancing board as claimed in claim 3, wherein the first wheel portion has a first tread pattern and the second wheel portion has a second tread pattern, the first tread pattern and the second tread pattern forming a combined tread pattern. 5. A self-balancing board as claimed in claim 4, wherein the second wheel portion can be conditioned between being fixed in angular position relative to the first wheel portion and being rotatable about the rotation axis relative to the first wheel portion to change the angular position of the first tread pattern relative to the second tread pattern to thereby change the combined tread pattern. 6. The self-balancing board of claim 1 , wherein the at least one auxiliary wheel extends longitudinally past a longitudinal edge of the platform. 7. A self-balancing board, comprising: a primary wheel assembly that has a first wheel portion and a second wheel portion sharing a rotation axis with the first wheel portion, the first wheel portion having a first tread pattern and the second wheel portion having a second tread pattern, the first tread pattern and the second tread pattern forming a combined tread pattern; a platform coupled to the primary wheel assembly, the platform having a first foot deck portion extending in a first direction from the at least one primary wheel assembly, and a second foot deck portion extending in a second direction from the primary wheel assembly; and at least one motor configured to drive the first wheel portion and the second wheel portion to rotate about the rotation axis, wherein the second wheel portion can be conditioned between being fixed in angular position relative to the first wheel portion and being rotatable about the rotation 

 axis relative to the first wheel portion to change the angular position of the first tread pattern relative to the second tread pattern to thereby change the combined tread pattern. 8. A self-balancing board as claimed in claim 7, wherein the first wheel portion and the second wheel portion are relatively independently rotatable, and wherein the at least one motor independently operates the first wheel portion and the second wheel portion to change the angular position of the first tread pattern relative to the second tread pattern. 9. A self-balancing board as claimed in claim 7, wherein the primary wheel assembly has an angular orientation fixing interface between the first wheel portion and the second wheel portion for fixing the second wheel portion in discrete angular positions relative to the first wheel portion. 10. A self-balancing board, comprising: a primary wheel; a platform coupled to the primary wheel, the platform having a first foot deck portion extending in a first direction from the primary wheel, and a second foot deck portion extending in a second direction from the primary wheel; an orientation sensor system coupled to the platform and configured to determine the orientation of at least one of the first foot deck portion and the second foot deck portion relative to horizontal; and at least two motors configured to drive the primary wheel at least partially based on sensor data from the orientation sensor system. 1 1. The self-balancing board as claimed in claim 10, wherein at least one of the at least two motors is external to the primary wheel. 12. The self-balancing board as claimed in claim 1 1 , wherein the at least two motors are external to the primary wheel. 13. The self-balancing board as claimed in claim 12, wherein a first of the at least two motors is positioned towards a first longitudinal end of the platform, and wherein a second of the at least two motors is positioned towards a second longitudinal end of the platform. 14. The self-balancing board as claimed in claim 13, wherein the first of the at least two motors drives the primary wheel via a first belt on one lateral side of the primary wheel and the second of the at least two motors drives the primary wheel by a second belt on another lateral side of the primary wheel.
